pattern: Two id-sorted lists combine like a merge step of merge sort: walk both fronts together, emit the smaller id, and add the values together only when the ids actually coincide.
hints: Both lists are already sorted by id with no duplicate ids inside either one -- that is exactly the setup for a merge-sort-style merge.
Walk two pointers, one per list. Whichever current id is smaller gets emitted on its own; when the ids are equal, emit one entry with the summed value and advance both pointers.
Do not forget the leftover tail: once one list is exhausted, copy the rest of the other list straight through unchanged.

edge_inputs.description: identical id sets, every id overlaps
single-element lists at the maximum id and value
single-element lists at the minimum id and value
one list's ids all sit below the other's, exhausting a pointer early
the other direction: the second list is exhausted first
perfectly interleaved, alternating ids with no overlap

stress.description: random unique ascending id lists of varying length and overlap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = rng.randint(1, 200)
    n2 = rng.randint(1, 200)
    pool = list(range(1, 1001))
    ids1 = sorted(rng.sample(pool, n1))
    ids2 = sorted(rng.sample(pool, n2))
    nums1 = [[i, rng.randint(1, 1000)] for i in ids1]
    nums2 = [[i, rng.randint(1, 1000)] for i in ids2]
    return [nums1, nums2]
